full_text:
Protocol for HGSOC tissue dissociation

Materials:

All the plastics are in the cupboard below the water bath or in the drawer next to laminar

Laminar hood

370C rotating incubator

Sterile surgical tools and scalpel

10cm tissue culture plates

Histology cassettes

70µm cell strainers

50mL falcon tubes

1.5mL / 2mL Eppendorf tubes

Cryovials

Cell freezing box

Liquid nitrogen

37-41% Formaldehyde

Dispase II stock 50U/mL (located in Färkkilä lab +40C)--> 20 ul / 1ml of the AdF+++

RQ1-RNAse free DNase (located in Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box) (50ul upto 5 ml or 100 ul/10 ml)

AdDF+++ medium: DMEM/F12 (500mL) + 1x Glutamax (5mL of 100x)+ 10mM HEPES (5mL 1M)+ Penstrep (5mL): Ready to use media will be in cell culture fridge.

RBC lysis buffer: cell culture fridge

Stem cell banker: cell culture fridge

Start date:

Preparation prior to sample collection

1. Bring liquid nitrogen (LN2).

2. Inside the fume hood, prepare 4 % formaldehyde solution (fixative) in a small plastic jar: 5 ml formaldehyde + 45 mL 1x PBS (non-sterile).

3. Bring ice, and switch on the 370C incubator.

4. Prepare the laminar hood as follows: Clean the surface with 70% EtOH, stick the plastic bag on to the left had glass wall for collecting the waste tissues and tips. Place the sterile, forceps, scissors, scalpel, and 10 cm tissue culture plate inside the laminar.

5. Prepare 2% virkon add 2 scoops of virkon powder into 1L bottle, fill up the bottle with 1L tap water, label with the date and let it dissolve (Do this when Anastasiya is not around).

6. Keep the lab phone with you all the time when Anastasiya is not around.

Protocol:

Sample ID: S036_p

Dissection at Näytehuone NKL

Place the MERKASOFT paper inside the hood. Place the tissue onto 10 cm plate; if the sample is too big transfer it to 15 cm plate. Take pictures with ruler using lab phone. Select the tumor region / nodule for snap freezing, formalin fixation and tissue processing. Preferably all from the same nodule. Trim away surrounding omentum tissue, and cut the tumor into 6 small pieces for snap freezing into LN2 2 piece for DNA, RNA, and protein isolation, freeze 1 piece per tube.

Dissect the nearest tissue for formalin fixation try to cut bigger area but small thickness so it fits well into the cassette. If there is normal looking omentum, cut away from the tumor nodules and transfer to 50 ml falcon, keep it on ice, bring it to lab for later transfer to -80. Transfer remaining tumor tissue into a jar with HBSS, keep it on ice and bring it to the lab.

2. Enzymatic dissociation:

2.1. In the ONCOSYS laminar hood, mince the bigger tumor piece (1-2 cm depending on the size of the sample) into smaller pieces using a sterile scalpel and or scissors; make sure to finely chop the tissue-using scalpel. If there are larger pieces difficult to filter in the next step and the yield will be smaller.

2.2. Transfer the minced tissue into 50 ml falcon tube containing 9.7 uL AdDF+++ + 200 uL of Dispase stock + 100 ul RNAse free DNase I. Reduce or increase the volume depending on the tissue size. Cover the lid with para film, and tape the tube inside 370C rotating incubator for 1 hr (incubator located in Färkkilä lab below the lab bench).

Note down the start time of enzymatic dissociation, started at…. am/pm

During 1hr incubation time do the following:

Transfer the tubes from LN2 to -80 box, keep the tissue block on a rocker. Place the enzymes back. Take out omentum gel, aprotinin, thrombin, and fibrinogen from the freezer. Thaw everything else but fibrinogen on ice, and fibrinogen in 37deg water bath. Cool cell culture centrifuge to +40C.

If Anastasiya is not around, save the paper you get from the clinic. Write the date and sample ID, and source of the tumor omentum/ovary/ peritoneum

Place dirty instruments in a plastic tip box near the sink; pour virkon on top of it. Pour virkon to the sample jar, remove the label on the jar and throw it away. Leave them for 2-3 hr or until you finish everything. If Anastasiya is not around pour the virkon waste to the sink, wash the instruments and the jar with tap water plus brush. Keep them for wash & autoclaving with a NOTE. If there are large tissue pieces left after virkon decontamination, put it in a tissue paper and discard it in the plastic bag placed inside the hood.

In the Väharautio’s lamilar, spray 70% EtOH to small white slide holder together with the transparent lid. Place it under the UV light for 30 min inside the laminar. ASK Erdogan’s help for UV sterilization.

Clean the surface of our laminar with 70% EtoH. Dip two shadon slides in 70% EtoH in a 50 ml falcon inside the laminar. Place it on top of dust free tissue for drying (inside the laminar).

Prepare organoid medium + Y+ IL2+IL1

Thaw IL2 and IL15 on ice. Dilute IL-2-> 1ul + 999 ul of sterile 0.1% BSA (in the cell culture fridge door).

Take 10-ml of ORG medium + Y, to this add 76 ul of diluted IL2 + 10ul of IL15

Mix well and keep the tube inside the laminar until use

3. Filtration and centrifugation

3.1. At the end of incubation bring the tube to laminar. Pre-label one 50mL falcon with sample ID, remove the cap, place 70µm cell strainer on top of 50mL falcon, pre-wet strainer with 1mL cold AdDF+++ medium. Transfer the digested suspensions through the strainer into 50mL tubes and wash the strainers with 5 mL above cold AdF+++. If the strainer gets clogged mix the tissue homogenate on top of the filter with 1ml tip.

3.2. Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC (Cell culture centrifuge).

NOTE: Always use cold AdF+++ medium, keep it on ice when not using.

4. Red blood cell lysis

4.1. Upon centrifugation from step 3.2., remove supernatant into a waste bottle containing virkon and resuspend the cell pellet in 2 mL- 4 mL RBC lysis buffer depending on the pellet size. Incubate at room temperature (RT) for 5 min. Add 10 (for 2 ml lysis buffer) -20 ml (for 4 ml lysis buffer) cold AdDF+++ mix well with the same pipette. Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC (Cell culture centrifuge).

5. Resuspension of the cell pellet viability measurement and downstream analysis

5.1. Carefully remove the supernatant into a waste bottle containing virkon, resuspend the cell pellet in 1-5 ml of cold AdF+++ medium.

NOTE: Decide the volume of resuspension depending on the pellet size. If the pellet is too large resuspension volume can be up to 5mL. Also dilute the bigger pellet 1:10 in AdF+++ before counting.

6. Viability analysis using Trypan Blue:

6.1. Take 10uL of cell suspension in a 1.5 ml tube add 10 ul of trypan blue mix well. Pipette 10uL per side x 2 sides of the LUNA cell-counting chamber. Note down the cell count & % viability from each side and take the average.

Cell count using LUNA cell counter

6.2. Calculate the amount of cells needed for organoid culture and FACS analysis, pipette the appropriate volume of cells into sterile 1.5 ml tube. (SEE NEXT PAGE FOR ORGANOID culture)

Proceed with the following step after setting up the culture: Once the organoid culture is set up and the cells and matrix look OK, add 5mL of AdF+++ to the remaining cell suspension, centrifuge 5 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4o(See below instructions for labelling). Place the tubes in a controlled cell-freezing box and transfer the box to -80 0C. If the pellet is small reduce the number of vial to 1 each for SC, ORG, and IF.

NOTE: First resuspend the cell pellet using 1ml stem cell baker with 1ml tip mix well, then add remaining 4 ml, and mix again and aliquot to cytotubes.

Print the labels using the labelling machine. Labelling example:

S036_pOme

Dispase

ORG / SC / IF

Cell count

Date

Initial

ORGANOID culture setup

Once you know the total cell count, calculate the volume of cell suspension needed for culturing in 24 or 96 well plate or shandon slides.

For example:

Average cell count: 8 x 106 cells / ml (If you have 5 ml cell suspension calculate accordingly (*5))

1. Tube 1 (24 wells, 100000 cells / well *10 wells)

2. Tube 2 (96 wells, 80000 cells / well * 5 wells)

3. Tube 3 (Shandon slides, 35000 cells / well *10 wells)

4. Tube 4 (FACS, 3000000 cells)

NOTE: If the volume of the cells is too low (below 100 ul), make up to 100 ul with AdF++ before spinning.

Quick spin all 1-3 tubes using microfuge on the lab bench for 1 min.

Resuspended the pellet in appropriate volume of organoid medium (see below) and proceed with the preparation of the gel.

Omentum gel for 24 well plate

Resuspend the pellet from the OME tube (tube 1) from step #2 in 323 ul of Organoid medium + Y, + IL-2 + IL15. To this add below reagents in the same order

316 ul omentum gel (2.5 mg /ml, 5.5 mg/ml stock)

45 ul aprotinin (33.3 ug/ ml, stock 500 ug/ml)

2 ul thrombin (0.3 Units, stock 100 units)

14 ul fibrinogen (0.5 mg/ml, stock 25 ug/ml)

mix it by pipetting up and down 5 times avoid bubbles. Quickly pipette 70 ul per well as a droplet (* 10 wells).

Omentum gel for 96 well plate

Pre-coat the wells with 30 ul of omentum gel (DO this while the cells are spinning during dissociation step).

Pre-wet the 96 wells using 50ul of ORG medium so that the omentum gel coating spreads nicely.

Pipette the coating mix as follows 30ul / well *5 wells

73.5 ORG medium

72.5 ul omentum gel (2.5 mg /ml, 5.5 mg/ml stock)

10.5 ul aprotinin (33.3 ug/ ml, stock 500 ug/ml)

0.45 ul thrombin (0.3 Units, stock 100 units)

3.2 ul fibrinogen (0.5 mg/ml, stock 25 ug/ml)

mix it by pipetting up and down 5 times avoid bubbles. Quickly pipette 30 ul per well, allow for solidification in the 37 incubator.

Once the coating is solidified, prepare cells + omentum gel mix as follows

115 ul cells in ORG medium

114 ul omentum gel (2.5 mg /ml, 5.5 mg/ml stock)

16 ul aprotinin (33.3 ug/ ml, stock 500 ug/ml)

0.7 ul thrombin (0.3 Units, stock 100 units)

5 ul fibrinogen (0.5 mg/ml, stock 25 ug/ml)

mix it by pipetting up and down 5 times avoid bubbles. Quickly pipette 50 ul per well, allow for solidification in the 37 incubator.

Omentum gel for shadon slides

115 ul cells in ORG medium

114 ul omentum gel

16 ul aprotinin (33.3 ug/ ml, stock 500 ug/ml)

0.7 ul thrombin (0.3 Units, stock 100 units)

5 ul fibrinogen (0.5 mg/ml, stock 25 ug/ml)

mix it by pipetting up and down 5 times. Quickly pipette 25 ul per well * 10 wells avoiding air bubbles.

Keep the slide on top of the slide box with sterile MQ at the bottom. Cover the lid of the box, transfer into 37 incubator for solidification.

Meanwhile complete the cell freezing process from step 6.2.

Once the gel has been solidified (aprox 20-30 min), add organoid medium + Y+ IL-2+IL-1 650 ul / 24 well, 150 ul / 96 well, and 30 ul /shadon well. Take pictures either before or after adding the medium

Keep the reagents back in -80 or +4.

NOTE: Remember to take the pictures on day 0 and on the day of harvesting / fixing. Better to take 10x, 20x, and 40x couple of each.

mL | %_viability | Average_size_um | Volume_mL
* 5 (ONLY if you have 5 ml) | Dilution *10 (ONLY if you dilute) | Total cells
